title: 3,000+ Tokens Per Second? Let's Stress Test Xiaomi's New Model: MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed
summary: Xiaomi and TileRT released a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model capable of exceeding 1,000 tokens per second on standard hardware. The architecture uses selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a TileRT persistent GPU kernel runtime to achieve these throughput levels.
writeup: Better Stack stress tests MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed, a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model from Xiaomi and TileRT that the summary credits with exceeding 1,000 tokens per second on standard hardware. The video covers the three techniques behind that throughput: selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and TileRT's persistent GPU kernel runtime, each aimed at reducing inference bottlenecks. The matter is inference speed at trillion-parameter scale, showing how quantization, speculative decoding, and a persistent kernel runtime combine to push tokens per second on commodity GPUs.
